# Lifestyle Medicine in Wayne, PA

## About Wayne, PA

Wayne, Pennsylvania is a vibrant Philadelphia Main Line community known for its walkable downtown, excellent schools, and family-friendly neighborhoods. Many residents choose personalized primary care that prioritizes prevention, longer visits, and strong doctor–patient relationships — which is the focus of Forward Family Medicine.

Wayne's walkable neighborhoods, proximity to Chester Valley Trail, and health-conscious community make it a natural fit for a practice that takes lifestyle seriously as medicine. Whether you're managing a chronic condition or investing in long-term prevention, Wayne is the right place to find a physician who thinks the same way.

## What Is Lifestyle Medicine?

Lifestyle medicine uses evidence-based behavioral interventions — changes in nutrition, physical activity, sleep, stress management, and social connection — as clinical tools to prevent and treat chronic disease. At Forward Family Medicine, these aren't "wellness extras." They're integrated into your care plan from day one.

Dr. Lieberman is dual board-eligible in Family Medicine and Lifestyle Medicine — the only such physician at any DPC practice on the Main Line.

[Full lifestyle medicine explainer →](/lifestyle-medicine)

## Why Wayne Patients Choose Lifestyle Medicine at Forward Family Medicine

- **Prevention is built in** — every visit includes a lifestyle lens, not just a prescription
- **Personalized nutrition counseling** — not a generic pamphlet, a real conversation about your diet
- **Exercise prescription** — specific, achievable, medically informed
- **Sleep taken seriously** — we screen and treat insomnia and sleep quality as a clinical priority
- **Stress management strategies** — evidence-based, not vague
- **Chronic disease focus** — diabetes, hypertension, cholesterol, obesity all benefit from lifestyle-first care
- **Lifestyle medicine included in membership** — no extra charge

## Services Included in Membership

- Lifestyle medicine consultations and follow-up
- Personalized nutrition and dietary pattern counseling
- Physical activity prescription and barrier coaching
- Sleep optimization and CBT-I based insomnia management
- Stress management and mind-body strategies
- Tobacco cessation support
- Weight management — comprehensive and evidence-based
- Chronic disease management through a lifestyle lens

[See full services →](/services)

## Practical Information

**Location:** Wayne, PA — serving the Main Line
**Telehealth:** Lifestyle medicine counseling is especially well-suited to phone and video visits. Members can have nutrition and coaching conversations without coming in.
**Chester Valley Trail:** Wayne is a short distance from the Chester Valley Trail — and we love when patients are already moving. We build on what you're already doing.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Is lifestyle medicine covered in the DPC membership in Wayne?**
Yes — completely. Lifestyle medicine counseling is integrated into every membership at no additional cost. [See what's included →](/membership)

**Is this alternative medicine or integrative medicine?**
Neither. Lifestyle medicine is conventional, evidence-based medicine. It uses the same clinical evidence standards as any medical specialty — it simply uses behavioral interventions as primary tools rather than afterthoughts. [Learn more →](/lifestyle-medicine)

**Can lifestyle changes actually treat my chronic condition?**
For many conditions — prediabetes, mild hypertension, high cholesterol, early metabolic syndrome — yes. Clinical evidence shows lifestyle interventions can match or exceed medication effects. We use medication when it's the right tool, but never instead of addressing root causes.

**Do I need to live in Wayne to access lifestyle medicine at Forward Family Medicine?**
No. Members from Berwyn, Devon, Villanova, Radnor, and surrounding Main Line communities are welcome. Telehealth makes lifestyle consultations accessible regardless of where you live.

# About Forward Family Medicine

## Hero

**Medicine the way it was always meant to be practiced.**

Forward Family Medicine was built on a simple conviction: primary care works best when a doctor has time to truly know the people they care for. That belief shapes everything we do — from how we schedule appointments to how we structure our practice.

---

## Our Story

Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman practiced traditional family medicine for [X] years and watched, year after year, as administrative burden and insurance constraints squeezed the time available for patients. A 15-minute appointment slot was never enough to do right by someone with diabetes, a new diagnosis, or a child who needed more than a quick look.

The Direct Primary Care model offered a different path — one where the doctor-patient relationship, not the billing code, is the center of care. Forward Family Medicine opened in Wayne, PA as a membership-based practice to give Main Line families exactly that: a physician who is genuinely available, genuinely present, and genuinely invested in their health.

## Our Approach to Care

### Relationship First
We keep our patient panel small by design. That means your doctor recognizes your voice on the phone, remembers what you talked about last visit, and has read your chart before you walk in the door.

### Whole-Person Medicine
We look beyond the chief complaint to understand what's happening in your life. Stress, sleep, nutrition, and relationships all affect your health, and we address them as part of your care plan.

### Lifestyle Medicine Integration
As a lifestyle medicine-informed practice, we use evidence-based behavioral interventions — nutrition counseling, physical activity guidance, sleep optimization, stress management — as first-line tools, not afterthoughts. [Learn more about our lifestyle medicine approach →](/lifestyle-medicine)

### Unhurried, Thoughtful Visits
Appointments are scheduled to fit your needs — not a billing clock. Whether you need 15 minutes for a quick concern or 60 minutes to review lab results and build a chronic disease management plan, we have time for you.

---

## Our Practice Philosophy in Three Sentences

We believe primary care should feel like having a trusted physician in the family. We believe prevention is more powerful than treatment. And we believe that when patients and doctors have time to build a real relationship, health outcomes improve — for everyone.

---

## Why Wayne, PA?

Wayne, Pennsylvania is a vibrant Philadelphia Main Line community known for its walkable downtown, excellent schools, and family-friendly neighborhoods. Many residents choose personalized primary care that prioritizes prevention, longer visits, and strong doctor–patient relationships — which is the focus of Forward Family Medicine.

We are proud to be part of the Wayne community and to serve families across the Main Line.

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Is Forward Family Medicine accepting new patients?**
Yes — we are currently accepting new members. We intentionally keep our panel small, so we encourage you to reach out before we reach capacity. [Schedule a free Meet & Greet →](/become-a-patient)

**Do you see children and adults?**
Yes. We care for patients of all ages, from newborns and children to adults and seniors.

**What if my needs are complex?**
The DPC model is well suited to complex, chronic cases because we have the time to manage them properly. We also coordinate with specialists and facilitate referrals when needed.

**Are you affiliated with a hospital?**
We maintain admitting privileges and referral relationships with local hospitals and specialists. [Contact us](/become-a-patient) for details on our hospital affiliations.

# Become a Patient at Forward Family Medicine

## Hero

**Your first step costs nothing — just a conversation.**

Before you commit to anything, we invite you to a free, no-obligation Meet & Greet with Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman. Ask every question you have, see the practice, and decide — on your timeline, with no pressure — whether Forward Family Medicine is the right fit for you and your family.

> **[Schedule Your Free Meet & Greet →](#schedule)**

---

## How to Get Started

### Step 1 — Schedule a Free Meet & Greet {#schedule}

A 20-minute conversation — in person or via video — with Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman. This is your chance to:

- Ask anything about the practice, the DPC model, and what membership includes
- Share your health history and current concerns to see if we're a good fit
- Tour the practice if you're coming in person
- Meet the team and get a feel for the culture

**No paperwork. No commitment. Just a conversation.**

> **Scheduling widget placeholder:**
> `[EMBED: Calendly or practice scheduling widget]`
>
> Or contact us directly:
> - **Phone:** [phone number placeholder]
> - **Email:** [email placeholder]
> - **Office hours:** [hours placeholder]

---

### Step 2 — Enroll Online

If you decide to become a member, enrollment is simple and takes about 10 minutes online.

1. Complete the enrollment form (personal information, health history, insurance details for reference)
2. Set up your monthly membership billing (ACH bank transfer or credit card)
3. Sign the membership agreement — a plain-language document explaining exactly what's included

> **Enrollment form link placeholder:** `[LINK: Patient enrollment portal]`

---

### Step 3 — Your First Comprehensive Visit

Once enrolled, we schedule your onboarding visit — a 60-minute appointment where we:

- Review your full health history in depth
- Discuss your health goals and any active concerns
- Order any overdue labs or screenings
- Build your personalized care plan, including lifestyle medicine components
- Set up your direct access to Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman

This is the most thorough first appointment most patients have experienced. Come with questions — we have time for all of them.

---

### Step 4 — You're a Member

From day one of membership, you have direct access to Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman by phone, text, and secure message. Same-day and next-day appointments are available. Your care has begun.

---

## Membership Plans at a Glance

| Plan | Who It's For | Monthly Fee |
|---|---|---|
| Individual Adult | Ages 18–64 | $[X]/month |
| Senior Adult | Ages 65+ | $[X]/month |
| Child | Ages 0–17 (with parent member) | $[X]/month |
| Family | 2 adults + up to 3 children | $[X]/month |
| Employer Group | 5+ employees | Contact us |

*One-time enrollment fee applies. Annual commitment or month-to-month options available.*

**[See full membership details →](/membership)**

---

## What to Expect at Your Meet & Greet

The Meet & Greet is informal and unhurried. Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman will ask about your current health situation and what brings you in, and you'll have time to ask about:

- How to transition from your current doctor
- What a typical year of membership looks like
- Whether your specific conditions and concerns fall within our scope
- How labs and medications are priced
- Anything else on your mind

There is no obligation at any point in the process. If you decide it's not the right fit, we'll part warmly and wish you well.

---

## Transitioning from Another Doctor

We make transitions easy:

- We request records from your previous provider on your behalf
- Ongoing prescriptions are reviewed and continued (where appropriate) from your first visit
- Specialist relationships are maintained — we work with your existing specialists, not against them
- We coordinate any outstanding referrals or care gaps from prior to enrollment

Most patients find the transition takes one or two weeks from enrollment to first visit, depending on how quickly records are available.

---

**Is the Meet & Greet really free?**

Forward Family Medicine offers concierge-style primary care on the Philadelphia Main Line — same-day access, direct physician contact, and unhurried visits — through an affordable DPC membership model.

# Concierge Medicine in Wayne, PA

**This Is Concierge Medicine. The Monthly Fee Just Doesn't Require a Second Mortgage.**

Forward Family Medicine is a concierge practice. Every member gets direct access to their physician, same-day appointments, unhurried visits, and a doctor whose panel is capped at 250 patients — so they actually have time for you. The membership is structured on the Direct Primary Care model, which means no insurance billing, predictable monthly pricing, and a practice built to keep your total cost of care low.

---

## What Is Concierge Medicine?

Concierge medicine (also called boutique medicine, retainer medicine, or personalized medicine) is an approach to primary care in which patients pay a membership or retainer fee in exchange for enhanced access and a deeper relationship with their physician.

The core promise of concierge medicine is simple: **a doctor with time for you.**

### Hallmarks of Concierge-Style Care
- Small patient panel — your doctor knows you by name and history
- Same-day or next-day appointment availability
- Direct phone, text, or email access to your physician
- Longer, more thorough appointments (30–60 minutes)
- Proactive, prevention-focused care — not just reactive sick visits
- Coordination with specialists and advocacy on your behalf
- 24/7 or after-hours urgent access to your own physician

Forward Family Medicine offers all of these — built on the Direct Primary Care (DPC) model.

---

## How Forward Family Medicine Compares

Traditional concierge medicine charges a premium retainer on top of — or instead of — insurance billing. The high price reflects both the quality of care and the overhead of maintaining an insurance billing department. Direct Primary Care removes the insurance overhead entirely, which is what allows FFM to deliver the same concierge experience at a fraction of the cost.

| | Traditional Concierge | Forward Family Medicine |
|---|---|---|
| Patient panel size | 300–600 patients | ~250 patients per physician |
| Monthly cost | $150–$500+/month | $200/month individual; family rates from $175 down to $100/person/month |
| Insurance billing | Often still billed | Never — no insurance billing for primary care |
| Visit length | 30–60 min | 1–2 hours |
| Direct doctor access | Yes | Yes — phone, text, email directly |
| Lifestyle Medicine | Rarely | Board-eligible, in-person, every visit |

Same experience. Lower overhead. Better medicine.

**[Learn more about how DPC works →](/direct-primary-care)**

---

## The Concierge Experience at Forward Family Medicine

### Your Doctor's Personal Attention
Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman maintain a purposefully small panel of members. That means every patient receives the kind of attention that simply isn't possible in a practice seeing 20–30 patients a day.

When you call, you speak directly with your doctor or a team member who knows your name. When you send a message, you receive a thoughtful response — not a portal notification pointing you back to schedule an appointment.

### Same-Day Access — Really
Urgent concern on a Monday morning? We aim for same-day availability for acute issues. Routine visits can typically be scheduled within 24 hours. For many questions, a quick phone call or message means you never need to come in at all.

### Visits Without a Clock
Appointments at Forward Family Medicine are scheduled to fit the conversation — not to hit a billing threshold. A routine medication refill might take 10 minutes. A new diagnosis, a complex chronic condition, or a patient who just needs to talk things through might take an hour. We make room for both.

### A Practice That Actively Lowers Your Costs

Concierge medicine has a reputation for being expensive. We think it should be the opposite. Because we know you well and you can reach us directly, we help you avoid the urgent care visits, redundant tests, and reactive ER trips that quietly inflate the cost of traditional care. We also dispense medications at cost and order labs at wholesale pricing — so when something is needed, it's not a financial event. The goal is for your total annual healthcare spend to go down as an FFM member, not up.

### Proactive Prevention
Concierge-style care means we do not wait for you to get sick to engage. We schedule preventive health reviews, track your labs over time, follow up on recommendations we've made, and reach out when we notice something worth discussing.


---

## Who Benefits Most from Concierge-Style DPC?

### Busy Professionals
When you can't afford to spend three hours waiting at an urgent care center, having same-day access to your own doctor — by phone, video, or in-person — changes everything.

### Families with Children
One membership can cover a family, and having a physician who knows your children's history (and yours) makes routine and urgent care far simpler and more reassuring.

### Adults Managing Chronic Conditions
Conditions like diabetes, hypertension, and thyroid disease require consistent, attentive management. The DPC model gives your doctor the time and continuity to manage these well — adjusting medications, reviewing labs, and checking in proactively.

### Anyone Who Has Felt Rushed or Dismissed
If you've ever left a doctor's appointment feeling like your questions weren't fully answered, or like the visit ended before you could say everything you needed to say, this practice was built for you.

---

## Serving the Philadelphia Main Line

Wayne, Pennsylvania is a vibrant Philadelphia Main Line community known for its walkable downtown, excellent schools, and family-friendly neighborhoods. Many residents choose personalized primary care that prioritizes prevention, longer visits, and strong doctor–patient relationships — which is the focus of Forward Family Medicine.

We serve Wayne, Berwyn, Devon, Malvern, Paoli, Radnor, Villanova, Newtown Square, and surrounding communities.

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Is concierge medicine worth it?**
Most patients who have experienced both traditional and concierge-style care say it is — particularly when the total cost (including copays, deductibles, and the value of time saved) is factored in. Our DPC model keeps the membership fee low enough that the math works for most families.

**Does Forward Family Medicine bill insurance?**
We do not bill insurance for primary care services. Your monthly membership fee covers all included services. You should maintain health insurance for specialist, hospital, and emergency care.

**How small is the patient panel really?**
We keep our panel purposefully limited to ensure every member receives the attention they deserve. Once the panel is full, we maintain a waitlist. We encourage prospective members to [reach out early →](/become-a-patient).

**Can I keep my current insurance and add this membership?**
Yes. Most members combine our DPC membership with their existing insurance (or switch to a high-deductible plan) for specialist and hospital coverage.

**What if I'm used to a large health system and want to keep those relationships?**
We work collaboratively with specialist physicians and large health systems. You do not need to abandon those relationships — you simply add a primary care physician who is far more accessible and engaged.

Concierge Medicine in Wayne, PA

This Is Concierge Medicine. The Monthly Fee Just Doesn’t Require a Second Mortgage.

Forward Family Medicine is a concierge practice. Every member gets direct access to their physician, same-day appointments, unhurried visits, and a doctor whose panel is capped at 250 patients — so they actually have time for you. The membership is structured on the Direct Primary Care model, which means no insurance billing, predictable monthly pricing, and a practice built to keep your total cost of care low.

What Is Concierge Medicine?

Concierge medicine (also called boutique medicine, retainer medicine, or personalized medicine) is an approach to primary care in which patients pay a membership or retainer fee in exchange for enhanced access and a deeper relationship with their physician.

The core promise of concierge medicine is simple: a doctor with time for you.

Hallmarks of Concierge-Style Care

  • Small patient panel — your doctor knows you by name and history
  • Same-day or next-day appointment availability
  • Direct phone, text, or email access to your physician
  • Longer, more thorough appointments (30–60 minutes)
  • Proactive, prevention-focused care — not just reactive sick visits
  • Coordination with specialists and advocacy on your behalf
  • 24/7 or after-hours urgent access to your own physician

Forward Family Medicine offers all of these — built on the Direct Primary Care (DPC) model.

How Forward Family Medicine Compares

Traditional concierge medicine charges a premium retainer on top of — or instead of — insurance billing. The high price reflects both the quality of care and the overhead of maintaining an insurance billing department. Direct Primary Care removes the insurance overhead entirely, which is what allows FFM to deliver the same concierge experience at a fraction of the cost.

Traditional ConciergeForward Family Medicine
Patient panel size300–600 patients~250 patients per physician

Insurance billingOften still billedNever — no insurance billing for primary care
Visit length30–60 min1–2 hours
Direct doctor accessYesYes — phone, text, email directly
Lifestyle MedicineRarelyBoard-eligible, in-person, every visit

Same experience. Lower overhead. Better medicine.

# What Is Direct Primary Care?

**Direct Primary Care is primary care freed from insurance billing — and built around the patient. At Forward Family Medicine, DPC isn't a budget alternative to concierge medicine. It's the financial structure that makes concierge-quality care available to everyone.**

In a Direct Primary Care (DPC) practice, patients pay a flat monthly membership fee directly to their physician. In return, they receive unlimited primary care services, direct access to their doctor, and longer, unhurried visits. No copays. No billing codes. No insurance middleman for everyday care.

---

## The Short Answer

**Direct Primary Care (DPC)** is a healthcare model in which:
1. Patients pay a predictable monthly membership fee directly to their physician ($200/month for individuals, less per person for families — reflecting a smaller panel and deeper level of care)
2. The practice does **not** bill insurance for primary care services
3. Patients receive **unlimited visits**, direct doctor access, and often **wholesale labs and medications**
4. The physician maintains a **smaller patient panel** — at FFM, capped at 250 patients per physician, versus 2,000+ in traditional practices

The result: your doctor has time for you.

---

## Why Does DPC Exist?

Traditional insurance-based primary care creates a structural problem: physicians are paid per visit, and payers (insurance companies) reimburse more for procedures than for thinking, listening, and preventing disease. To cover overhead, a primary care physician in a traditional practice needs to see 20–30 patients per day.

In that environment, appointments get compressed to 10–15 minutes. Preventive conversations get cut short. Chronic conditions get managed reactively. And patients feel like a number.

DPC solves this by removing insurance billing from primary care entirely. The practice revenue comes from membership fees, so the physician's incentive is aligned with the patient's health — not with billing volume.

---

## What DPC Covers at Forward Family Medicine

### Included in Membership
- Unlimited office visits (in-person, phone, video)
- Same-day or next-day scheduling
- Direct physician access (call, text, message)
- Annual physicals and preventive screenings
- Acute illness care (infections, injuries, etc.)
- Chronic disease management
- Prescription refills and medication management
- Lifestyle medicine and health coaching
- Minor in-office procedures

### At-Cost Add-Ons
- Laboratory work (ordered at wholesale/near-cost pricing)
- Medications (dispensed at cost through direct supplier relationships — no pharmacy markup)
- Immunizations (at cost)

### Not Covered by DPC Membership
- Specialist visits and procedures (billed through your insurance)
- Hospital care and emergency services
- Imaging (X-ray, MRI — we help coordinate and advocate for you)

**[See the full services list →](/services)**

---

## How DPC Is Different from Traditional Primary Care

| | Direct Primary Care | Traditional Primary Care |
|---|---|---|
| Payment | Monthly membership fee | Insurance + copays + deductibles |
| Visit length | 1–2 hours | 10–15 min (typical) |
| Doctor access | Direct (call/text/message) | Via scheduling + nurse line |
| Wait for appointment | Same or next day | Days to weeks |
| Patient panel size | ~250 patients per physician (at FFM) | 1,500–2,500 patients |
| After-hours access | Your own doctor | Answering service / urgent care |
| Preventive focus | Central to every visit | Often squeezed out |

---

## DPC vs. Concierge Medicine — What's the Difference?

The distinction most practices draw between DPC and concierge medicine doesn't apply at Forward Family Medicine. Every member receives what the industry calls a "concierge experience" — a physician who knows you, a panel capped at 250 patients, same-day access, and unhurried appointments — delivered through the DPC financial model: no insurance billing, predictable monthly pricing, and a deliberate focus on keeping your total cost of healthcare as low as possible.

Most DPC practices run panels of 400–600 patients. We cap ours at 250 — because that's what it actually takes to practice the way we believe medicine should be practiced.

**[Learn more about our concierge medicine features →](/concierge-medicine)**

---

## What We Do to Lower Your Total Cost of Care

The DPC model removes insurance billing from primary care. We go further. At Forward Family Medicine, fiscal responsibility is built into how we practice — because a doctor who actually knows you is also a doctor who can help you avoid the healthcare costs that shouldn't happen in the first place.

- Labs at wholesale pricing — CBC: $2 | CMP: $2 | Lipid panel: $3 | ApoB: $7 | Thyroid panel: $7 | and more. These are the prices you pay. Not what insurance "negotiates."
- Medications dispensed at cost through direct supplier relationships — no pharmacy markup
- Same-day access that prevents unnecessary urgent care and ER visits — for many acute issues, a quick call or message to your doctor is all you need
- Discounted imaging coordination — we work with local imaging centers to get members reduced rates on X-rays, CTs, and MRIs
- No per-visit fees, ever — your membership covers every visit, every call, every message

The goal is simple: you should spend less on healthcare every year as an FFM member than you would in the traditional system — and get far more for it.

---

## Do I Still Need Health Insurance with a DPC Membership?

Yes — we recommend it. Your DPC membership covers primary care comprehensively, but it does not cover:
- Emergency room visits
- Hospital stays
- Surgery
- Specialist consultations
- Imaging and advanced diagnostics

Most DPC members pair their membership with a **high-deductible health plan (HDHP)** or a **health-sharing ministry plan** for these situations. Because your primary care is covered by membership, you actually *use* your high deductible less often — meaning lower total healthcare costs overall.

A rough comparison for a healthy adult:

| Option | Monthly Cost (est.) |
|---|---|
| Traditional PPO plan only | $400–$600+/month |
| DPC membership + HDHP | $150–$250/month (combined) |

*Estimates vary widely by age, employer, and plan. Consult a benefits advisor for your specific situation.*

---

## Is DPC Right for You?

DPC works especially well for:

- **Individuals and families without employer-sponsored insurance** who want excellent primary care at a predictable cost
- **People with chronic conditions** (diabetes, hypertension, thyroid disorders) who need ongoing, attentive management
- **Preventive health-focused patients** who want more time with their doctor to work on root causes
- **Self-employed individuals and small business owners** looking for a cost-effective health benefit
- **Families who want one doctor** who knows every member

DPC may be less ideal if you primarily need specialist-heavy or hospital-based care, or if your employer's insurance plan already fully covers primary care at very low cost.

---

## DPC on the Philadelphia Main Line

Direct Primary Care is growing across the United States, and the Philadelphia Main Line is no exception. Families in Wayne, Berwyn, Devon, Malvern, and surrounding communities increasingly seek out the DPC model for its transparency, accessibility, and emphasis on relationship-centered care.

Wayne, Pennsylvania is a vibrant Philadelphia Main Line community known for its walkable downtown, excellent schools, and family-friendly neighborhoods. Many residents choose personalized primary care that prioritizes prevention, longer visits, and strong doctor–patient relationships — which is the focus of Forward Family Medicine.

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**What does "direct" mean in Direct Primary Care?**
"Direct" refers to the direct financial relationship between patient and physician — you pay your doctor directly, without an insurance company in the middle. This removes administrative overhead and aligns incentives around your health, not billing volume.

**Is DPC covered by Medicare or Medicaid?**
DPC membership fees are not covered by Medicare or Medicaid. However, Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries can join a DPC practice as cash-paying members. Specialty and hospital services would still be billed through Medicare or Medicaid as applicable.

**How is DPC regulated?**
DPC is recognized and legal in all 50 states. Many states have passed specific DPC legislation affirming that a DPC membership agreement is not an insurance product and does not require an insurance license to operate.

**Does the monthly fee really cover everything in primary care?**
It covers the vast majority of what most people need from a primary care physician. You only pay extra for labs, medications, and immunizations — and even then, at cost.

**How do I know if this practice is a good fit before joining?**
That's exactly what our free Meet & Greet is for. [Schedule one here →](/become-a-patient)

## About Direct Primary Care

**What is Direct Primary Care (DPC)?**
Direct Primary Care is a membership-based model of primary care in which patients pay a flat monthly fee directly to their physician — bypassing insurance for primary care services. In exchange, members receive unlimited visits, direct physician access, and longer appointments. The physician maintains a small patient panel — at Forward Family Medicine, capped at 250 patients per physician, versus 2,000+ in traditional practices — which is what makes personalized, accessible care possible. [Full DPC explainer →](/direct-primary-care)

**How is DPC different from concierge medicine?**
Both models offer smaller panels, direct access, and longer visits. The key difference: traditional concierge medicine typically charges a higher annual retainer and may still bill insurance. DPC does not bill insurance for primary care and charges a lower flat monthly fee — making it more accessible. [See the full comparison →](/concierge-medicine)

**Is DPC legal?**
Yes. DPC is legal in all 50 states. Many states have passed legislation explicitly affirming that DPC membership agreements are not insurance products and do not require an insurance license to operate.

**How many patients does your physician see per day?**
We schedule based on need, not volume. On a typical day, Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman may see 6–12 patients — compared to 20–30 in a traditional practice. This is what enables longer appointments, same-day access, and follow-through on care.

---

## Membership & Pricing

**What does a membership cost?**
Individual membership is $200/month. Family membership starts at $175/person/month for two members and scales to $100/person/month for five or more. There is no enrollment fee. [See full membership details and pricing →](/membership)

**What does the monthly membership include?**
Your membership covers unlimited primary care visits (in-person, video, and phone), direct physician access, preventive care, chronic disease management, lifestyle medicine counseling, same-day/next-day scheduling, and minor in-office procedures. Labs and medications are available at wholesale/near-cost pricing as separate add-ons. [See full service list →](/services)

**Are there any copays or per-visit fees?**
No. Once you are a member, there are no additional fees for covered visits. The only charges beyond your membership are for labs, medications, and immunizations — all priced at cost.

**Is there a long-term commitment?**
We offer both month-to-month and annual membership options. Month-to-month plans require 30 days' written notice to cancel. Annual plans include a modest discount.

**Is there an enrollment fee?**
At Forward Family Medicine, there is no enrollment fee.

**Can I use my HSA or FSA for membership fees?**
HSA/FSA eligibility for DPC membership fees depends on your specific plan and applicable tax rules. Many health-related expenses at our practice (labs, medications, procedures) are eligible for HSA/FSA reimbursement. Please consult your benefits administrator or tax advisor.

**Do you offer employer group memberships?**
Yes. We work with small and mid-size employers to offer DPC memberships as a health benefit. [Contact us to discuss employer options →](/for-businesses)

---

## Insurance

**Do I still need health insurance if I join a DPC practice?**
Yes — we recommend it. Your DPC membership provides comprehensive primary care but does not cover hospital stays, emergency care, specialist visits, surgery, or imaging. We strongly recommend maintaining a health insurance plan (ideally a high-deductible health plan paired with an HSA) for these scenarios.

**Will you bill my insurance for visits?**
No. We do not bill insurance for primary care services. Your membership fee is the only charge for covered visits. Some members choose to submit their own claims for out-of-network reimbursement — check with your insurer.

**Is DPC covered by Medicare or Medicaid?**
DPC membership fees are not reimbursed by Medicare or Medicaid. Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries are welcome to join as self-pay members. Specialist and hospital services would still be billed through Medicare or Medicaid as appropriate.

**Can I use my insurance for labs and imaging ordered by your practice?**
If you have insurance, you can choose to use it for labs and imaging. However, our wholesale lab pricing is often lower than your insurance-adjusted rate — we'll help you compare and choose.

---

## Appointments & Access

**How quickly can I get an appointment?**
Members typically get same-day or next-day appointments for acute concerns. Routine visits can be scheduled within 24–48 hours. Many concerns are resolved by phone or message without any in-person visit.

**Can I contact my doctor between visits?**
Yes. Members have direct access to Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman via phone, text, and secure messaging. You will not reach an answering service or a nurse line.

**What are your office hours?**
Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM For urgent matters, members have after-hours contact access for true clinical urgency (not prescription refills or routine requests).

**What if I have a medical emergency?**
Always call 911 or go to the nearest emergency room for life-threatening emergencies. Your DPC membership is for primary care — not emergency or hospital care.

**Do you offer telehealth visits?**
Yes. Phone and video visits are included in your membership. Many concerns — prescription refills, minor illness, result review, follow-up — can be handled without coming in.

---

## Services & Scope

**Do you see children?**
Yes. We provide care for patients of all ages, from newborns through older adults. Children can be added to a family membership. [See membership options →](/membership)

**Can you manage my chronic conditions?**
Yes. Chronic disease management — diabetes, hypertension, thyroid disease, cholesterol, asthma, and more — is a core part of what we do. The DPC model is particularly well-suited for chronic disease management because it provides the time and continuity these conditions require.

**Do you provide mental health care?**
We screen for and treat common mental health conditions including depression and anxiety, and provide medication management. For therapy and psychiatric care, we maintain strong referral relationships and help coordinate access.

**Can you be my primary care physician even if I have specialists?**
Absolutely. We work alongside your specialists and serve as the coordinating physician for your overall care — reviewing specialist notes, reconciling medications, and keeping your care integrated.

**Do you do physicals and well-woman exams?**
Yes. Annual physicals, well-woman exams, and preventive health screenings are core services included in your membership.

**What procedures can you do in-office?**
We perform a range of minor procedures: EKGs, ear lavage, wound care, joint injections, skin tag removal, suturing, spirometry, and more. [See full procedures list →](/services)

---

## Lifestyle Medicine

**What is lifestyle medicine and is it included in my membership?**
Lifestyle medicine uses evidence-based behavioral interventions — nutrition, physical activity, sleep, stress management — to prevent and treat chronic disease. It is integrated into every membership at no additional cost. [Learn more →](/lifestyle-medicine)

**Do you have a specific diet you recommend?**
We don't prescribe one-size-fits-all diets. We use evidence-based guidance — drawing on Mediterranean, DASH, whole-food plant-based, and other well-studied approaches — tailored to your health goals and preferences.

---

## Getting Started

**How do I become a patient?**
The first step is a free, no-obligation Meet & Greet with Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman. [Schedule yours here →](/contact)

**What happens at the Meet & Greet?**
It's a 20-minute conversation — in person or by video — where you can ask questions, learn about the practice, and decide whether Forward Family Medicine is right for you. No pressure, no commitment.

**How long does enrollment take?**
Online enrollment takes about 10 minutes. Your first comprehensive visit is typically scheduled within the first week or two of enrollment.

**What do I need to bring to my first appointment?**
A list of your current medications, any recent lab results or medical records, and any questions or concerns you'd like to address. We'll have reviewed your submitted health history before you arrive.

---

## Still Have Questions?

We're happy to help. [Contact us directly →](/contact) or schedule a Meet & Greet and ask in person.

**[Schedule a Free Meet & Greet →](/contact)**

# Forward Family Medicine — Primary Care Built Around You

## Hero

**Your doctor should know your name — not just your chart.**

Forward Family Medicine is a Direct Primary Care practice in Wayne, PA, where a flat monthly membership replaces the rushed, insurance-driven office visit with something far better: a real relationship with your family doctor.

## What We Offer

Membership at Forward Family Medicine covers the full scope of primary care — with no copays, no surprise bills, and no waiting months for an appointment.

- **Unlimited primary care visits** — in-person, by phone, or via video
- **Same-day or next-day appointments** — because your health can't always wait
- **Direct access to your doctor** — call, text, or message anytime
- **Preventive care & chronic disease management** — from annual physicals to diabetes, hypertension, and more
- **Lifestyle medicine** — personalized plans addressing nutrition, movement, sleep, and stress
- **Wholesale labs & medications at cost** — we pass the savings directly to you

---

## How Membership Works

Forward Family Medicine runs on a simple model: you pay one flat monthly fee, and we provide all the primary care you need. No insurance billing. No per-visit charges. No surprise costs.

| What you get | What you skip |
|---|---|
| Unlimited visits | Copays and deductibles |
| Direct doctor access | Long hold times |
| Wholesale labs & medications | Surprise bills |
| Longer, unhurried appointments | 7-minute office visits |

Most members pair their DPC membership with a high-deductible health plan or health-sharing plan for specialist and hospital coverage — keeping total healthcare costs lower than traditional insurance alone.

**[See full membership details →](/membership)**

---

## Services Snapshot

We handle the full range of primary care needs.

- Annual physicals & wellness exams
- Preventive screenings & immunizations
- Acute illness & injury care (ear infections, sprains, UTIs, and more)
- Chronic disease management (diabetes, hypertension, thyroid, cholesterol)
- Mental health support & care coordination
- Lifestyle medicine consultations
- Minor in-office procedures
- Specialist referrals & care coordination

**[See all services →](/services)**

---

## Why Patients Choose Forward Family Medicine

### Access — When You Need It
Members reach their doctor directly — by phone, text, or secure message — without going through a call center. Same-day and next-day appointments are the norm, not the exception.

### Relationship — A Doctor Who Knows You
With a smaller patient panel, your physician has the time to understand your full picture: your history, your goals, your family, and your life. Visits run 30–60 minutes when you need them to.

### Prevention & Lifestyle — Getting Ahead of Disease
We go beyond treating symptoms. Our lifestyle medicine approach addresses the root causes of chronic illness — nutrition, movement, sleep, and stress — so you can feel better now and avoid problems later.

### Transparency — No Billing Surprises
One predictable monthly fee covers your primary care. Labs and medications are available at wholesale cost. You always know what your healthcare will cost.

---

## Serving Wayne, PA and the Philadelphia Main Line

Wayne, Pennsylvania is a vibrant Philadelphia Main Line community known for its walkable downtown, excellent schools, and family-friendly neighborhoods. Many residents choose personalized primary care that prioritizes prevention, longer visits, and strong doctor–patient relationships — which is the focus of Forward Family Medicine.

We proudly serve patients in Wayne, Berwyn, Devon, Malvern, Paoli, Radnor, Villanova, Newtown Square, and surrounding Main Line communities.

**Do I still need health insurance with a DPC membership?**
We recommend keeping a catastrophic or high-deductible plan for hospitalizations, specialist care, and emergencies. Your DPC membership covers primary care — often at a much lower total cost than traditional insurance copays and deductibles alone.

**How quickly can I get an appointment?**
Members typically get same-day or next-day appointments. Many questions can be handled the same day by phone or message without coming in at all.

Yes. Chronic disease management is a core part of what we do. Conditions like diabetes, hypertension, thyroid disorders, and high cholesterol are well-suited to the DPC model because ongoing care is fully included in your membership.

# Lifestyle Medicine at Forward Family Medicine

## Hero

**Most chronic diseases are preventable — and many are reversible — with the right approach.**

Lifestyle medicine is an evidence-based medical specialty that uses therapeutic changes in nutrition, physical activity, sleep, stress, substance use, and social connection to prevent, treat, and often reverse chronic disease. At Forward Family Medicine, these tools are built into every membership — not offered as an expensive add-on.

---

## What Is Lifestyle Medicine?

Lifestyle medicine is a branch of medicine that recognizes a fundamental truth: the way we live profoundly affects our health. The six pillars of lifestyle medicine — established by the American College of Lifestyle Medicine — are:

1. **Whole-food, plant-predominant nutrition** — eating patterns that reduce inflammation and chronic disease risk
2. **Physical activity** — regular movement as medicine, tailored to your abilities and goals
3. **Restorative sleep** — sufficient, quality sleep as a cornerstone of metabolic and cognitive health
4. **Stress management** — evidence-based techniques to reduce the physiological burden of chronic stress
5. **Avoidance of risky substances** — tobacco cessation, alcohol reduction, and related support
6. **Social connection** — the documented health impact of relationships, community, and belonging

These are not "soft" wellness concepts. They are well-studied clinical interventions. The evidence base for lifestyle medicine spans thousands of peer-reviewed studies — and for many conditions, lifestyle changes outperform medication alone.

---

## Why Lifestyle Medicine Belongs in Primary Care

Most chronic diseases seen in a family medicine practice — type 2 diabetes, hypertension, obesity, high cholesterol, fatty liver disease, anxiety, insomnia — are strongly driven by lifestyle factors. Yet in traditional primary care, a 10-minute visit rarely allows for a meaningful conversation about nutrition, sleep, or stress.

At Forward Family Medicine, our DPC membership model gives us the time to have those conversations. We treat lifestyle as a clinical tool, not a side note.

> *"We can prescribe the best medications in the world, but if we don't address what's driving the disease, we're always playing catch-up. Lifestyle medicine lets us get ahead of it."* — Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman

---

## How We Use Lifestyle Medicine at Forward Family Medicine

### At Your First Visit
During your comprehensive onboarding visit, we assess not just your labs and vitals, but your daily patterns — what you eat, how you sleep, how you move, and what stressors are active in your life. This forms the foundation of a personalized health plan.

### Ongoing Integration
Lifestyle factors are woven into every visit, not siloed into a separate "wellness" appointment. When we review your A1C or blood pressure, we're also asking about sleep quality, meal patterns, and stress levels — because all of it is connected.

### Individualized Plans
We do not hand you a generic pamphlet. We work with you to build realistic, sustainable changes that fit your life — your schedule, your preferences, your family, and your baseline.

### Tracking Progress Over Time
Because you have a continuous relationship with your doctor (not a different face every visit), we track your lifestyle interventions over months and years, adjusting the plan based on what's actually working.

> **Image placeholder:** `/src/assets/stock/lifestyle-consult.jpg`
> *Alt text: Physician and patient reviewing nutrition and lifestyle plan — lifestyle medicine at Forward Family Medicine Wayne PA*

---

## Conditions We Address with Lifestyle Medicine

Lifestyle interventions are most powerful for — but not limited to — the following conditions:

- **Type 2 diabetes and prediabetes** — nutrition and activity interventions can reduce A1C significantly, sometimes eliminating the need for medication
- **Hypertension (high blood pressure)** — dietary changes (DASH/Mediterranean), sodium reduction, and exercise have clinically proven effects
- **Hyperlipidemia (high cholesterol)** — plant-based nutrition patterns and weight management can produce meaningful improvements
- **Obesity and metabolic syndrome** — personalized, sustainable weight management without fad diets
- **Cardiovascular disease prevention** — addressing modifiable risk factors proactively
- **Anxiety and mild-to-moderate depression** — physical activity, sleep optimization, and stress management as evidence-based adjuncts
- **Insomnia** — behavioral sleep medicine (CBT-I principles) before reaching for medication
- **Chronic fatigue and low energy** — often rooted in sleep, nutrition, or stress factors amenable to change
- **Fatty liver disease (NAFLD)** — diet and activity are the primary treatment

---

## Our Approach to Nutrition

We do not prescribe a single "correct" diet. Instead, we use the best available evidence — which consistently points toward:

- **Plenty of vegetables, fruits, legumes, and whole grains**
- **Adequate lean protein** (plant and/or animal sources depending on your preferences and health goals)
- **Minimizing ultra-processed foods, refined sugar, and excess sodium**
- **Eating patterns that are sustainable for you long-term**

We are familiar with and can help you navigate a range of evidence-informed dietary approaches: Mediterranean, DASH, whole-food plant-based, low-glycemic, time-restricted eating, and others. The best diet is the one you can actually follow.

---

## Physical Activity as Medicine

Exercise is one of the most powerful interventions available for chronic disease prevention, mood, metabolic health, and longevity. We work with you to build a movement plan that:

- Meets current physical activity guidelines (150+ min/week moderate activity)
- Is appropriate for your current fitness level and any limitations
- Combines aerobic activity with resistance/strength training
- Addresses barriers — because most people know they should exercise more, they just need help making it realistic

We are not personal trainers — we are physicians who understand the clinical evidence and can translate it into practical guidance.

---

## Sleep as a Clinical Priority

Sleep is chronically underemphasized in traditional medicine. At Forward Family Medicine, we treat sleep as a vital sign. Poor sleep is linked to obesity, diabetes, hypertension, immune dysfunction, cognitive decline, and mental health conditions.

We screen for:
- Sleep duration and sleep quality
- Obstructive sleep apnea (and refer for sleep studies when indicated)
- Circadian disruption (shift work, irregular schedules)
- Behavioral factors driving insomnia

We use behavioral approaches (CBT-I principles) as first-line treatment for insomnia before defaulting to sleep medications.

---

## Stress and the Body

Chronic psychological stress has measurable physiological effects: elevated cortisol, immune suppression, worsened glycemic control, increased cardiovascular risk, and more. We take stress seriously as a clinical variable — asking about it directly, validating its impact, and offering concrete strategies including:

- Mindfulness and relaxation techniques
- Cognitive-behavioral approaches
- Exercise and sleep as stress buffers
- Referrals to mental health professionals when appropriate

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Is lifestyle medicine the same as alternative medicine or integrative medicine?**
No. Lifestyle medicine is conventional, evidence-based medicine that uses behavioral and lifestyle interventions — all of which have a strong scientific evidence base. It sits firmly within mainstream medicine and is a recognized specialty of the American Board of Lifestyle Medicine (ABLM).

**Will you just tell me to eat salad and exercise?**
No. We provide individualized, practical, evidence-based guidance tailored to your specific health situation, goals, and life circumstances. One-size-fits-all advice is not part of what we do.

**Can lifestyle changes actually replace medication?**
For some patients with some conditions — particularly prediabetes, mild hypertension, and early metabolic syndrome — lifestyle interventions can achieve results comparable to (or better than) medication, and may allow for medication reduction or elimination. We are honest about when medication is the right tool and when lifestyle is — and often, both work together.

**Is lifestyle medicine covered by my DPC membership?**
Yes — lifestyle medicine counseling and coaching are integrated into your Forward Family Medicine membership at no additional cost. [See full membership details →](/membership)

**Do I have to make dramatic changes overnight?**
Absolutely not. We work incrementally. Small, sustainable changes over time are far more effective — and more durable — than dramatic interventions that don't stick.

---

See how lifestyle medicine fits into your care plan.

Lifestyle Medicine at Forward Family Medicine

Hero

Most chronic diseases are preventable — and many are reversible — with the right approach.

Lifestyle medicine is an evidence-based medical specialty that uses therapeutic changes in nutrition, physical activity, sleep, stress, substance use, and social connection to prevent, treat, and often reverse chronic disease. At Forward Family Medicine, these tools are built into every membership — not offered as an expensive add-on.

What Is Lifestyle Medicine?

Lifestyle medicine is a branch of medicine that recognizes a fundamental truth: the way we live profoundly affects our health. The six pillars of lifestyle medicine — established by the American College of Lifestyle Medicine — are:

  1. Whole-food, plant-predominant nutrition — eating patterns that reduce inflammation and chronic disease risk
  2. Physical activity — regular movement as medicine, tailored to your abilities and goals
  3. Restorative sleep — sufficient, quality sleep as a cornerstone of metabolic and cognitive health
  4. Stress management — evidence-based techniques to reduce the physiological burden of chronic stress
  5. Avoidance of risky substances — tobacco cessation, alcohol reduction, and related support
  6. Social connection — the documented health impact of relationships, community, and belonging

These are not “soft” wellness concepts. They are well-studied clinical interventions. The evidence base for lifestyle medicine spans thousands of peer-reviewed studies — and for many conditions, lifestyle changes outperform medication alone.

# Membership at Forward Family Medicine

## Hero

**One simple monthly fee. Unlimited primary care. No surprises.**

Membership at Forward Family Medicine gives you direct access to a physician who knows you, answers when you call, and has time to help you get and stay well — for a flat monthly fee that's typically less than what most people pay in insurance copays and deductibles each year.

---

## What Membership Includes

Your monthly membership covers the full spectrum of primary care:

### Visits & Access
- Unlimited in-office visits — no per-visit fee, ever
- Same-day and next-day appointments for acute concerns
- Telehealth visits (video and phone) included
- Direct messaging, texting, and phone access to your doctor
- After-hours urgent questions handled by your own physician (not an answering service)

### Preventive & Chronic Care
- Annual physical exams and wellness visits
- Preventive health screenings (per USPSTF guidelines)
- Immunizations (at cost)
- Chronic disease management — diabetes, hypertension, thyroid, asthma, cholesterol, and more
- Medication management and refills

### Lifestyle Medicine
- Personalized nutrition, movement, sleep, and stress counseling
- Health coaching integrated into your care plan
- Behavioral health support and care coordination
- [Learn more about lifestyle medicine →](/lifestyle-medicine)

### Cost-Saving Benefits
- Lab work at wholesale/near-cost pricing (significant savings vs. billed rates)
- Prescription medications at cost through our direct dispensing partnerships
- Specialist referral coordination — we advocate for you
- No insurance billing overhead = savings passed to you

---

## How Membership Works {#how-it-works}

### Step 1 — Schedule a Free Meet & Greet
A 20-minute, no-obligation conversation with Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman to ask questions, tour the practice, and decide if Forward Family Medicine is right for you.

### Step 2 — Enroll & Complete Your Health History
Enrollment takes about 10 minutes online. You'll complete a health history form and we'll request records from your previous provider.

### Step 3 — Schedule Your First Comprehensive Visit
New members receive a thorough onboarding visit — typically 45–60 minutes — where we review your full health history, establish your care goals, and build a personalized prevention plan.

### Step 4 — You Have Direct Access to Your Doctor
From day one, you can reach Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman directly by phone, text, or message — and schedule same-day or next-day appointments whenever you need care.

---

## Membership vs. Traditional Insurance: A Comparison

| | Forward Family Medicine | Traditional Insurance |
|---|---|---|
| Monthly cost | Flat membership fee | Premiums + copays + deductibles |
| Visit time | 30–60 min typical | 7–15 min typical |
| Doctor access | Direct (phone/text/message) | Via scheduling + call center |
| Wait for appointment | Same or next day | Days to weeks |
| Lab costs | Wholesale (near cost) | Billed at insurance rates |
| Annual total (est.) | Predictable | Unpredictable |

> **Important:** DPC membership is **not** a replacement for health insurance. We strongly recommend maintaining a high-deductible health plan (HDHP) or health-sharing plan for hospital, specialist, and emergency coverage. Many members find their total healthcare spend decreases significantly when they combine DPC membership with an HDHP.

---

## Is Membership Tax-Deductible?

DPC membership fees may be deductible as medical expenses under IRS guidelines if you itemize deductions, and may be eligible for use with a Health Savings Account (HSA) in some circumstances. Tax laws change — please consult your tax advisor for guidance specific to your situation.

---

## Why Patients Choose Membership Over Traditional Care

> *"I was skeptical at first — pay a monthly fee on top of insurance? But once I added up my copays, lab bills, and the hours I wasted on the phone with a nurse line, the math was obvious. I actually saved money and got dramatically better care."*
> — **Michael R., Wayne PA** *(placeholder)*

> *"As a small business owner, I offered DPC memberships to my team instead of a traditional health plan. Sick days dropped, morale went up, and everyone has a doctor they actually like."*
> — **Business owner, Berwyn PA** *(placeholder)*

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Is there a long-term commitment?**
We offer both month-to-month and annual membership options. Annual plans include a modest discount.

**What happens if I need to see a specialist?**
We coordinate specialist referrals for you and stay in communication with consulting physicians. Specialist visits and procedures are billed separately through your insurance.

**Can I use my HSA or FSA?**
Many of our members use HSA or FSA funds for eligible expenses (labs, medications, procedures). The deductibility of membership fees as HSA-qualified expenses is subject to federal law and your specific plan — consult your benefits administrator.

**What if I need care while traveling?**
You can reach Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman by phone or message no matter where you are. For emergencies, always go to the nearest emergency room.

**Is this available for employer groups?**
Yes. We work with small and medium-sized employers to offer DPC memberships as a benefit. [Contact us for employer pricing →](/become-a-patient#employer)

**How do I cancel?**
Members may cancel with 30 days' written notice. There are no long-term contracts required for our month-to-month plan.

# The Patient Experience at Forward Family Medicine

## Hero

**Primary care that feels like it used to — and better than you expected.**

Being a member at Forward Family Medicine is a different kind of healthcare experience. No waiting rooms full of strangers. No hold music. No 8-minute appointments. Just a physician who knows you, is available when you need them, and has the time to actually help.

Here's what membership looks like in real life.

---

## From the First Conversation

### The Free Meet & Greet
Before you become a member, we invite you to a free, no-obligation Meet & Greet with Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman. This is a 20-minute conversation — in the office or by video — where you can:

- Ask anything about the practice and the DPC model
- Share your health history and current concerns
- Understand exactly what membership includes and how it works
- See the practice space and meet the team
- Decide, with no pressure, whether it's the right fit

Most prospective members say the Meet & Greet answered questions they didn't even know they had. Many leave having already decided to join.

> **[Schedule your free Meet & Greet →](/become-a-patient)**

---

## Becoming a Member

Enrollment is straightforward:

1. **Complete your enrollment form online** — takes about 10 minutes
2. **Set up your membership billing** — one flat monthly charge, no hidden fees
3. **Submit your health history** — via our secure patient portal
4. **Request records from your previous provider** — we help facilitate this

Once enrolled, you receive access to Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman's direct contact information. From day one, you can reach out by phone, text, or message.

---

## Your First Comprehensive Visit

New members receive a **60-minute onboarding visit** — the most thorough first appointment most patients have ever had.

We cover:
- Your full personal and family health history
- Current medications and supplements
- Preventive health needs and overdue screenings
- Your goals — not just your diagnoses
- Any active concerns you want to address
- A personalized health plan, including lifestyle medicine components

Patients regularly tell us this was the first time a doctor asked about their sleep, their stress, their diet, and what they actually want from their healthcare.

---

## Day-to-Day Membership: What Access Really Means

### When Something Comes Up
A sore throat on Tuesday morning. A question about a medication side effect. A child who woke up with an ear infection. A blood pressure reading that looks off.

As a member, you don't call a general office line and wait. You send a message or call Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman directly. You get a response the same day — often within hours. If you need to be seen, you're in by end of day or the next morning.

Compare this to the typical experience: calling a practice, being put on hold, speaking to a nurse who takes a message, waiting for a callback, and then being told the next available appointment is in two weeks.

### When You Come In for a Visit
The waiting room at Forward Family Medicine is quiet. There is no double-booking, no overflowing schedule. When you have an appointment, it starts on time.

Visits are as long as they need to be. A medication refill or a quick concern might take 15 minutes. A new diagnosis or a preventive health review might take 45–60 minutes. There is no pressure to hurry.

### Between Visits
You can reach Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman by text, message, or phone for any clinical question. Lab results are reviewed with you directly — not just released to a portal without explanation. Follow-up is proactive: if a concern needs monitoring, we reach out to you, not the other way around.

> *Alt text: Patient and physician in a relaxed, warm conversation — Forward Family Medicine Wayne PA*

---

## Appointments That Feel Different

In a traditional practice, a physician seeing 25 patients a day is spending an average of 15 minutes per person — and much of that time is documentation, not conversation.

At Forward Family Medicine, a typical appointment looks like this:

**You arrive.** The office is quiet and familiar. The staff know your name.

**The visit starts on time.** Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman has reviewed your chart before you walk in — you don't need to repeat your history from scratch.

**You have time to talk.** All of your questions get answered. If something comes up mid-visit that needs more time, we make room for it.

**You leave with a clear plan.** What we found, what it means, and what comes next — explained in plain language.

**You have a way to follow up.** If you have a question at home, you know how to reach Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman directly.

---

## For Families

Membership is designed to work for whole families — not just individuals. A family membership covers parents and children under one plan.

For families, the DPC experience means:
- One physician who knows every family member's history
- After-hours urgent questions handled without a trip to urgent care
- No scrambling to find a sick-day appointment
- A doctor who notices patterns across the family and connects the dots

## For People with Chronic Conditions

If you live with a chronic condition — diabetes, hypertension, thyroid disease, anxiety, or something else requiring ongoing management — the DPC model offers something traditional medicine rarely does: time and continuity.

Your condition is managed with the full context of your life. Medication adjustments happen promptly, not at the next available quarterly slot. When you have a flare or a concern between visits, you reach your own doctor — who already knows your full history.

Many members with chronic conditions report better lab values, fewer complications, and — importantly — feeling genuinely supported in managing their health over time.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Is the experience really that different from a traditional practice?**
Yes — meaningfully so. The most consistent feedback from members is that they had forgotten what it felt like to have a doctor who knows them and has time for them. The DPC model makes this structurally possible in a way that traditional insurance-based medicine cannot.

**What if I need to be seen quickly and my doctor is unavailable?**
Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman builds same-day capacity into the schedule every day. For true emergencies, we direct you to the appropriate care setting. For urgent-but-not-emergency concerns when Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman is unavailable, we have a coverage plan in place and will communicate it to you clearly.

**Can I still see specialists I've been seeing for years?**
Yes. Your DPC membership is for primary care. Your specialist relationships remain completely intact. We coordinate with your specialists and can serve as a communication hub.

**Is the office accessible and easy to get to?**
Yes. We're located in [address placeholder], with convenient parking. [See directions →](/become-a-patient#location)

We use a HIPAA-compliant messaging platform for secure communication. Details are provided at enrollment. In practice, most members use a combination of text, direct call, and portal message — whatever works best for them.

The Patient Experience at Forward Family Medicine

Hero

Primary care that feels like it used to — and better than you expected.

Being a member at Forward Family Medicine is a different kind of healthcare experience. No waiting rooms full of strangers. No hold music. No 8-minute appointments. Just a physician who knows you, is available when you need them, and has the time to actually help.

Here’s what membership looks like in real life.

From the First Conversation

The Free Meet & Greet

Before you become a member, we invite you to a free, no-obligation Meet & Greet with Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman. This is a 20-minute conversation — in the office or by video — where you can:

  • Ask anything about the practice and the DPC model
  • Share your health history and current concerns
  • Understand exactly what membership includes and how it works
  • See the practice space and meet the team
  • Decide, with no pressure, whether it’s the right fit

Most prospective members say the Meet & Greet answered questions they didn’t even know they had. Many leave having already decided to join.

Schedule your free Meet & Greet →

Becoming a Member

Enrollment is straightforward:

  1. Complete your enrollment form online — takes about 10 minutes
  2. Set up your membership billing — one flat monthly charge, no hidden fees
  3. Submit your health history — via our secure patient portal
  4. Request records from your previous provider — we help facilitate this

  • Preventive health needs and overdue screenings
  • Your goals — not just your diagnoses
  • Any active concerns you want to address
  • A personalized health plan, including lifestyle medicine components

primary care for adults Wayne PA

# Primary Care Services at Forward Family Medicine

## Hero

**Comprehensive primary care — all included in your membership.**

Forward Family Medicine provides the full breadth of family medicine, from your annual physical to complex chronic disease management. All included services are covered by your flat monthly membership fee. No copays. No billing surprises.

---

## Preventive Care & Wellness

Prevention is at the heart of what we do. We follow evidence-based USPSTF guidelines and work proactively to help you avoid chronic disease before it starts.

- **Annual physical exams** (comprehensive history, physical, and lab review)
- **Well-woman exams** (Pap smears, breast health, reproductive health)
- **Well-child visits and pediatric care** (newborn through adolescence)
- **Preventive health screenings** (blood pressure, cholesterol, diabetes, colorectal cancer, STI screening, and more)
- **Immunizations** (adult and pediatric vaccines, flu shots, travel vaccines — at cost)
- **Periodic health reviews** — proactive check-ins between annual visits
- **Health risk assessments** — personalized prevention planning based on family history and risk factors
- **Advance care planning** — goals of care, advance directives

---

## Acute & Urgent Care

When you're sick or injured, you need your doctor — not a walk-in clinic with no knowledge of your history. Members receive same-day or next-day access for acute concerns.

- **Respiratory infections** (colds, flu, bronchitis, sinusitis, COVID)
- **Ear infections and sore throats** (strep testing and treatment)
- **Urinary tract infections** (UTIs)
- **Skin conditions** (rashes, infections, wound care)
- **Minor injuries** (sprains, strains, lacerations)
- **Gastrointestinal illnesses** (nausea, diarrhea, stomach pain)
- **Allergic reactions** (mild to moderate)
- **Eye and ear concerns** (pink eye, ear pain)
- **Urgent medication needs**

> **Note:** For life-threatening emergencies, always call 911 or go to the nearest emergency room. We handle urgent-but-not-emergency concerns that would otherwise send you to an urgent care center or ED.

---

## Chronic Disease Management

Chronic disease management thrives in the DPC model — because managing a condition well requires time, continuity, and a physician who knows your full history. All chronic disease management visits are included in your membership.

- **Type 2 diabetes and prediabetes** — A1C monitoring, medication management, lifestyle counseling
- **Hypertension (high blood pressure)** — medication management, dietary counseling, monitoring
- **Hyperlipidemia (high cholesterol)** — statin and non-statin management, lifestyle intervention
- **Hypothyroidism and hyperthyroidism** — lab monitoring, medication titration
- **Asthma and COPD** — inhaler management, action plans, monitoring
- **Obesity and metabolic syndrome** — comprehensive, evidence-based weight management
- **Osteoporosis and bone health** — screening, supplementation, bisphosphonate management
- **Anxiety and depression** — medication management, behavioral health support, referrals
- **ADHD** — adult ADHD evaluation and medication management (age/scope dependent — confirm with practice)
- **Autoimmune conditions** — co-management with specialists
- **Chronic pain** — multimodal management, coordination with pain specialists

---

## Women's Health

- Annual well-woman exams
- Pap smears and cervical cancer screening
- Breast health evaluation and screening referrals
- Contraception counseling and management
- Menopause and perimenopause care
- Osteoporosis prevention and management
- Preconception counseling
- Postpartum care coordination

---

## Men's Health

- Annual exams and preventive screenings
- Testosterone and hormonal health evaluation
- Cardiovascular risk assessment
- Prostate health screening and counseling
- Sexual health
- Mental health and behavioral health support

---

## Mental Health & Behavioral Health

- Screening for depression, anxiety, and other common conditions
- Medication management for depression and anxiety
- Brief counseling and support
- Referrals to therapists, psychiatrists, and behavioral health specialists
- Care coordination with mental health providers

---

## Lifestyle Medicine

- Personalized nutrition counseling
- Physical activity prescription and guidance
- Sleep optimization and insomnia management
- Stress management strategies
- Tobacco cessation support
- Substance use counseling and referrals
- Weight management — comprehensive and evidence-based

**[Learn more about our lifestyle medicine approach →](/lifestyle-medicine)**

---

## Minor In-Office Procedures

The following minor procedures are included in membership where clinically appropriate, or available at minimal cost:

- EKG (electrocardiogram)
- Ear lavage (earwax removal)
- Skin tag and wart removal
- Wound care and suturing (select lacerations)
- Joint injections (cortisone)
- Ingrown toenail treatment
- Urinalysis, rapid strep testing, rapid flu testing
- Urine pregnancy testing
- Spirometry (lung function testing)

---

## Lab & Diagnostic Testing

Laboratory work is ordered through our wholesale lab partnerships — meaning you pay near-cost pricing, not inflated billed rates. Common labs cost a fraction of what you'd pay through insurance.

**Example savings (approximate):**

| Test | Typical insured rate | Forward FM member rate |
|---|---|---|
| Complete metabolic panel | $40–$100+ | $6–$10 |
| Lipid panel | $30–$80+ | $4–$7 |
| HbA1c | $25–$60+ | $4–$6 |
| Thyroid panel (TSH) | $40–$100+ | $8–$12 |
| Complete blood count | $25–$60+ | $3–$5 |

*Rates are approximate and subject to change. Imaging and advanced diagnostics are not included in membership — we help you navigate these efficiently.*

---

## Care Coordination & Referrals

Your primary care physician is your advocate across the healthcare system. We coordinate:

- Specialist referrals (cardiology, orthopedics, dermatology, OB/GYN, neurology, and others)
- Pre-surgical evaluations and clearances
- Hospital follow-up care
- Communication with specialists and review of consultant notes
- Medication reconciliation after specialist visits or hospitalizations
- Advance care planning

We stay in the loop so that your care is integrated, not fragmented.

---

## Telehealth & Remote Care

All telehealth is included in membership. Many concerns can be resolved without ever coming in:

- Video visits (scheduled)
- Phone consultations
- Secure messaging for non-urgent questions
- Prescription refills and medication management
- Lab result review and counseling
- Post-visit follow-up

---

## Services Not Included in Membership

The following are not included in DPC membership and would be billed separately through your insurance or paid out-of-pocket:

- Emergency room care and hospitalization
- Specialist visits and procedures
- Imaging (X-ray, MRI, CT, ultrasound) — we assist with ordering and finding cost-effective options
- Physical therapy, occupational therapy, chiropractic
- Dental and vision

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Do you see children?**
Yes. We provide pediatric care from newborns through adolescence. A child membership can be added to a parent's family plan. [See membership details →](/membership)

**Are lab fees included in membership?**
Lab fees are separate but priced at wholesale/near-cost. You will always pay far less than standard billed rates.

**Do you prescribe controlled substances?**
We prescribe controlled substances for appropriate clinical indications (e.g., ADHD medication, pain management in limited circumstances) in accordance with applicable laws and clinical guidelines.

**What if I need imaging?**
We order imaging and work to find cost-effective options for our members. We can direct you to facilities with transparent, competitive cash pricing when insurance does not cover it.

**What's the best way to know if you can help with my specific condition?**
[Schedule a free Meet & Greet →](/become-a-patient) and ask us directly. We are happy to tell you what falls within our scope and how we'd approach your care.

---
